main.note: main.cjs sets preload to '../preload/preload.mjs', but electron.vite.config.ts builds preload.cjs (electron/preload.cjs). In dev electron-vite uses electron.vite.config.ts; verify preload path for packaging.

renderer.note: No direct use of 'window.desktop', 'window.api', or 'ipcRenderer' was detected in these files. UI currently runs with in-process React state and local event handlers.

stateManagementSummary.details: App uses React useState in src/App.tsx for workspace and UI state; state is passed as props to child components. No Redux, MobX, or global central store detected.

risks: Preload path mismatch between main.cjs and electron.vite.config.ts may cause packaged app preload not found.
If new Blueprint wrappers require native actions (save, run, open), preload currently only exposes 'ping' — additional API surface will be required.
No IPC usage in renderer reduces risk: wrappers can be implemented without changing existing IPC contracts.
recommendations: Do not change existing IPC payloads; implement wrappers that forward props/events to existing handlers.
If additional native actions are needed, extend preload with a minimal, strongly-typed API (e.g., desktop.runQuery, desktop.openConnection) and add corresponding main handlers.
Align preload path in main.cjs to electron/preload.cjs (or ensure packaged build includes preload at the configured path) to avoid runtime failures after packaging.
conclusion: Renderer currently does not depend on IPC; state is local React state and will be preserved by wrapper components. Proceeding with the Blueprint UI refactor is low-risk regarding IPC, but verify preload path parity for packaging.
